pytorch_task_v1.prompt_instructions: Implement this ML task as a SINGLE self-contained Python file (task.py). Include all required functions AND a complete `if __name__ == '__main__':` block that trains the model, evaluates on a validation split, prints standard metrics (MSE, R2, parameter accuracy where applicable), and asserts quality thresholds so the script exits non-zero on failure. Do NOT create separate test files or README. The script itself is the test.
evaluation_rules: The `evaluate()` function MUST compute and return standard metrics: MSE, R2 score, and any task-specific metrics on the VALIDATION data split.

The `if __name__ == '__main__':` block MUST: (1) train the model, (2) evaluate on BOTH train and validation splits, (3) print all metrics clearly, (4) assert quality thresholds (e.g., R2 > 0.9, MSE < threshold), (5) exit 0 on success or non-zero on failure.
Do NOT use a separate test file. All evaluation logic goes in main().

tasks.algorithm: CUDA Core vs Tensor Core GEMM Benchmark
tasks.description: Measure and explain the performance difference between a 'traditional CUDA-core' GEMM path (FP32, TF32 disabled) and a 'modern Tensor Core' GEMM path (FP32 input/output, TF32 compute) using a simple fully-connected layer. Sweep matrix sizes from 512x512 to 8192x8192. Also provide an equivalent CUDA C++ cuBLAS benchmark using cublasSgemm (baseline) and cublasGemmEx / cuBLASLt with TF32 Tensor Ops (Tensor Core path). Use GPU-side timing via CUDA Events with warm-up iterations. Report average latency (ms) and compute throughput (TFLOPS). Save a multi-panel comparison plot.

benchmark_modes: Mode 1 — FP32 CUDA Core baseline: torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = False
Mode 2 — TF32 Tensor Core path: torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True
cublas_equivalent: Tensor Core: cublasGemmEx with CUBLAS_COMPUTE_32F_FAST_TF32 and CUBLAS_GEMM_DEFAULT_TENSOR_OP
requirements.timing: GPU-side CUDA Events (torch.cuda.Event); warm-up=20, bench=100 iterations; torch.cuda.synchronize() before timing starts.
requirements.visualization: 4-panel PNG: (1) latency bar chart (log scale), (2) throughput bar chart, (3) speedup line chart with annotations, (4) latency scaling line chart.
validation: TF32 average throughput >= FP32 average throughput on Tensor Core-capable GPUs (Volta, sm_70+).
Average TF32 speedup > 1.0x on Tensor Core-capable GPUs.
requirements.additional_deliverable: cublas_gemm_benchmark.cu — a self-contained CUDA C++ file compilable with: nvcc -O3 -arch=sm_80 cublas_gemm_benchmark.cu -lcublas -o cublas_gemm_bench
